<size=36>Spicy Kelp will burn th' entire row of zombies upon contact. Blow me down! Ye flounderin' nincompoop.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Drown) + 1800 (Cremator)</color></size>

plants.name: Strangle Kelp
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Strangle Kelp stands out as a true philosopher among plants. It often ponders profound questions like, "Where does th' grass come from, and whar does it go?" However, its disregard for personal hygiene and unkempt hair make other plants shy away, preferring to keep their distance.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Strangle Kelp can drag three zombies into th' water. Sling yer hook!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Drown) ×3</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Drags three zombies into th' water. Batten down the hatches! Each drag also deals 600 damage to broadside zombies.</color></size>

plants.name: Kelp-spreader
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Kelp-spreader basked in th' sun, his eyes scanning th' horizon. "Many plants be scared of me gaze," he chuckled. "But they'd understand if they had to wear three pairs of sunglasses for protection like I do!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can fire kelps that slow down aquatic zombies. Blisterin' barnacles!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s + Insta-kill (Drown)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Each attack adds 1 Tangle value. Thundering typhoons!</color>
<color=#006400>Tangled Debuff:</color><color=#8B0000>Slows targets and stackable with Chill. Zombies affected receive more and more additional percentage damage from kelps based on th' zombie's Tangle Value, until they're insta-killed when th' Tangle Value be full (30).</color></size>

plants.name: Infernowood
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"It's sweltering out here!" Infernowood fumed. Blow me down! Avast ye! Convinced that th' intense weather was to blame for its skin, it noted how th' flames on its head had also turned a vibrant red. Maybe it was th' perfect time to kick off a summer adventure called th' "Sledding Tour."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Infernowood can provide an even stronger damage boost to projectiles! Fifteen men on a dead man's chest!

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Explodes like a Jalapeno upon death. Yo ho ho.
<color=black>•</color>Ignites projectiles for th' following effects: Batten down the hatches! Dead men tell no tales.
-Peas, Potatoes and Low-tier Ignited Peas: Blisterin' barnacles! +40 damage
-Hypno Peas: +30 damage
-Blaze Peas: +30 damage, turn into phoenix peas
-Snow Peas: Turn into regular peas
-Cherries, Iron Peas, Suns: ×4 damage, Sun shots now drop 15 Suns instead of 5.
<color=black>•</color>Deals 40 damage-over-time to broadside zombies every 1.5s. Ye flounderin' nincompoop.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: Sling yer hook!<color=#8B0000>Torchwood + Jalapeno + Jalapeno</color>

plants.name: Scorchwood
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>High temperatures and spiciness always go hand in hand. Scorchwood may not grasp this concept, but his years of working at th' barbecue stall have certainly infused him with a distinctive heat.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Projectiles ignited by th' Scorchwood has greatly increased power. Ye flounderin' nincompoop.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Explodes like a Jalapeno upon death.
<color=black>•</color>Ignites projectiles for th' following effects:
-Peas, Potatoes and Low-tier Ignited Peas: Blimey! Cleave him to the brisket! +30 damage
-Snow Peas: Savvy? Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum! Turn into regular peas. Ye flounderin' nincompoop.</color></size>

plants.name: Spicy Squash
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm not as impatient as me brother," Spicy Squash stated calmly. Sling yer hook! "He rushes through everything, but I've learned that true success comes from patience and careful observation. Sometimes, it's best to wait and let th' fire simmer."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Spicy Squash can burn a whole row of zombies.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Single tile, Squash) + 1800 (Lane, Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range:</color><color=#8B0000>2 tiles to th' left; 3 tiles to th' right</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Releases Jalapeno effect when crushing zombies. Blow me down!</color></size>

plants.name: Torchthree
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Why be Torchthree so rich?" th' plants frequently pondered. "Oh, it's nay big deal," he shrugged. Torchthree then produced three peas from his hair and added, "I used to work as a magician in a circus."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Splits flaming peas into at most 3 lanes.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
Ignites peas: Fifteen men on a dead man's chest!
<color=black>•</color>1st ignition be exactly like Torchwood;
<color=black>•</color>2nd ignition splits into two peas on th' rows above and below;
<color=black>•</color>3rd ignition splits into three peas across all three rows;
<color=black>•</color>Th' cycle repeats.</color></size>

plants.name: Torch-kelp
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Fire struggles to exist beneath th' water, which means I can't use me flames to light th' way," Torch-kelp admitted. "But once above th' surface, me flames can shine once again."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Ignites kelp shots and drags one zombie into th' water.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Drown)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Ignites peas and kelp shots for +20 damage. Ignited kelp shots can add 1 Tangle Value to all zombies in th' splash range. Thar she blows! Splice the mainbrace! For every 100 shots lit, a Roasted Kelp be spawned in front.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: Splice the mainbrace!<color=#8B0000>Torchwood > Tangle Kelp</color>

plants.name: Roasted Squash
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Roasted Squashes jump out from th' Squash Torchwood one by one, "It's getting hot in there; our entire body be melting!" they declared.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Just a Squash grilled and glazed with delicious sap. Sling yer hook!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range:</color><color=#8B0000>0.5 tiles to th' left; 1 tile to th' right</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Cannot be used for fusions.
<color=black>•</color>Can be converted to a regular Squash using an Ice Pot.</color></size>

plants.name: Scorched Threepeater
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Why does Jalapeno always get all th' credit?" Scorched Threepeater exclaimed, smoke escaping from his mouth. Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum! "If they only realized that fusing with Jalapeno burns your peas." He turned to Torchwood with a serious look. "Trust me, ye wouldn't want to munch on charred peas!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>A Threepeater who can't fuse because he's scorched. Batten down the hatches! Dead men tell no tales. Arrr! Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Produces a line of flames that deal 1800 (Cremator) damage in three lanes when planted or fused.</color></size>

plants.name: Squashwood
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"It's way too hot! Too hot!" Squash yelled, trapped in th' flames of Torchwood. "Who put me here? I should be squashing zombies, not being th' punchline at a barbecue party!" th' heat was overwhelming, and as he reached his breaking point, he ignited, bursting forth from th' torch to rejoin th' battle against th' zombies.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces a Squash in front for each 60 peas that pass through it.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>For every 60 peas lit, a Roasted Squash be spawned in front.</color></size>

plants.name: Spikerock
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Spikerock just got back from a trip to Europe. He had a great time, met some wonderful people, really broadened his horizons. He never knew they made museums so big, or put so many paintings in them. That was a big surprise for him. Savvy?</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Spikerocks pop multiple tires and hurt any zombies that step on them.

<color=black>Damage: Shiver me timbers!</color><color=#8B0000>20×2/1s</color>
<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>450</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Can only be placed on existing Spikeweeds. Can puncture tires, but deducts 50 Toughness. Loses 50 Toughness when slammed on by Gargantuars and loses 150 when stomped on by Mecha Gargs.</color></size>

plants.name: Torchweed
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm Torchweed, th' zombies' passionate barbecue chef! Ignite that little pea, and I'll throw them an unforgettable barbecue party. Rest assured, I'm a professional—I guarantee it will be crispy on th' outside and tender on th' inside!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Torchweed can ignite small peas and puncture vehicles.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40/1 Second</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Ignite small peas for increased, and splashed, damage.
<color=black>•</color>For every 100 small peas lit, a Roasted Spikeweed be spawned in front.
<color=black>•</color>Can also pop th' balloons of regular Balloon Zombies.</color></size>

plants.name: Scorchweed
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Studies reveal that spiciness be actually a type of pain," Scorchweed announced, rattling th' red thorns on his head. "I intend to let th' unbelieving zombies experience this reality for themselves."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Scorchweed pops tires and enflames zombies that step on them.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40/1 Second</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Attacks inflict Enflamed Debuff. Scorchweeds can be upgraded to Hearthrocks using a Fertilizer.</color>
<color=#720026>Enflamed Debuff:</color><color=#8B0000>Damage taken increases by 50%; zombies with this debuff explode on death, spreading it further.</color></size>

plants.name: Squashweed
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Huh? Yeah." This catchphrase belongs to Squashweed, who rarely speaks and often leaves others feeling uncertain. After dinner, th' topic of thorns always becomes a favorite among th' plants. It's said that on an ordinary afternoon, as Squash prepared to crush a broadside zombie, he overlooked th' thorns lurking beneath its feet. Blow me down!</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Zombies will be knocked back every time it attacks.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Each attack will knock back zombies and fire 1 bouncing mini-squash.</color></size>

plants.name: Weed-spreader
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Weed-spreader rose to fame on Tumbler with his viral posts on weeding techniques, but when fans tried them near their lawnmowers, jammed engines and smoking hoods ensued. "Effective, not vehicle-safe," he shrugged.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires 3 weeds that deal enhanced damage to vehicles.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>5×3/1.5s (Piercing)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Weeds deal 30% maximum Toughness damage to vehicles at th' cost of themselves. Dead men tell no tales.</color></size>

plants.name: Gatling Pea-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"We're off to join th' army!" th' three little mushrooms shouted with joy. Although their heights didn't meet th' requirements and locating small helmets was a struggle, these challenges couldn't hold back th' Gatling Pea-shrooms. Yo ho ho. United as one, they set their sights on becoming th' top trio in th' army.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can be stacked three times to shoot 12 small peas at once!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×4/2.25s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles). Dead men tell no tales.</color></size>

plants.name: Hydra Kelp
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Hydra Kelp possesses a contemplative heart. If ye hand him a glass of root beer, be prepared for a discourse on Nietzsche's or Kant's works.Although he has profound musings, sometimes he longs for lighthearted conversations.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires kelps that bounce upon contact and slow down water zombies, can also drag 3 zombies under th' water.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×3/3s + Insta-kill (Drown) ×3</color>
<color=#006400>Tangled Debuff:</color><color=#8B0000>Kelps bounce upon contact, dealing 40 damage to broadside zombies when they land. Each kelp 1 Tangle Value upon contact.</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Can drag 3 zombies under th' water.</color></size>

plants.name: Cattail
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Woof!" says Cattail. "Woof woof woof! Dead men tell no tales. Does this confuse you? Do ye expect me to say 'Meow' like a cat because th' word 'cat' be in me name and I also look like a cat? That's not how things work around here. I refuse to be pigeonholed."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cattails can attack any lane and shoot down balloon zombies.

<color=black>Damage: Blimey!</color><color=#8B0000>20×2/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Can only be placed on existing Lily Pads.</color></size>

plants.name: Frost Cattail
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm not just a cat... I'm... Savvy? I'm a super blue cat!" declared Frost Cattail. "Do ye have some cold catnip...?" His love for catnip shines through, and he has an undeniable fascination with all things cool.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Frost Cattail can attack any zombie in th' lane and deal extra damage to chilled targets.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×2/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Deals ×2 damage to chilled zombies. Fifteen men on a dead man's chest! Deals ×4 damage to frozen zombies.</color></size>

plants.name: Flame Cattail
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"When I was young, I thought I'd be a regular Cattail," Flame Cattail said, warming her tail with pride. "Turns out, I'm a blazing inferno of awesomeness!" Frost Cattail sighed from th' corner, muttering, "Showoff."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Flame Cattail can attack zombies in any lane and deal high damage.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×2/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Deals ×3 damage to Enflamed zombies.</color></size>

plants.name: Gloom-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I've always enjoyed releasing heavy fumes," says Gloom-shroom. "I know a lot of people aren't cool with that. They say it's rude or that it smells bad. All I can say is, would ye rather have your brain eaten by zombies?"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Gloom-shrooms fire heavy fumes in an area around them.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×4/1.9s (Piercing)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Can only be placed on existing Fume-shrooms. Blisterin' barnacles! Ye flounderin' nincompoop.</color></size>

plants.name: Flame Gloom-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Flame Gloom-shroom's ability to release hot steam has established him as a celebrated pastry baker. His signature spicy pastries be a favorite among many, cherished for their distinct flavors. Sling yer hook!</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Releases high-damaging fire fumes around it.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×4/1.9s (Piercing)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Inflicts th' Enflamed debuff when attacking zombies.</color></size>

plants.name: Frost Gloom-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Dreaming of a career as a physicist, th' Frost Gloom-shroom dedicates his days to unraveling th' complexities of th' world, avoiding contact with other plants. "Why do I have multiple strings of icicles sprouting from me head?" he muses, th' latest question occupying his thoughts. He's oddly adamant about calling them "strings."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Releases cold fumes around that can freeze zombies.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×4/1.9s (Piercing)</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System:</color><color=#8B0000>Each attack applies Chill and adds 5 Cryo points.</color></size>

plants.name: Flame Tall-nut
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Flame Tall-nut be a huge fan of th' Olympics and passionately insists that Torchwood be chosen as th' Olympic torch. Cautious warning to th' uninformed though: Do not try to eat him. Ye may find yourself burning up from th' spicy heat.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Zombies will get enflamed when it be bitten.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>8000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>When affected by th' Jalapeno, heals 3000 Toughness. Inflicts Enflamed debuff and deals 20 damage to zombies that bite him.</color></size>

plants.name: Icerock
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After his return from Alaska, Icerock couldn't contain his excitement. Eager to freeze th' zombies solid, he relished th' thought of "ice" bursting beneath their feet. He made it a point that everyone use th' term "ice." His skin still carried th' chill from his Arctic adventure, and his eyes shimmered with th' brilliance of unmelting snow.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can puncture tires and freeze zombies.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>450</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×2/1s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System:</color><color=#8B0000>Attacks inflict Chill and add 5 Cryo points.</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Inherits traits from Spikerock. Blimey! Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen!</color></size>

plants.name: Hearthrock
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Not long ago, Hearthrock traveled to Mexico and was swept away by th' vibrant enthusiasm of th' locals, smartly falling in love with tacos. Now, he's pouring all his energy into a taco museum alongside Icerock, though Icerock doesn't seem to share his enthusiasm.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Enflame zombies that step on it and also puncture vehicles. Batten down the hatches!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>450</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×2/1s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Inherits traits from Spikerock. Applies th' Enflamed Debuff when attacking zombies. Cleave him to the brisket!</color></size>

plants.name: Urchin-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"People often ask about me appearance, so let me clarify: I'm from th' Namib Desert," Urchin-shroom said to anyone who inquired. "If you're familiar with geography, you'll know that a desert can be found right next to th' ocean!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attacks flying zombies and airships, growing in size after a while.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20 → 20×4/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Growth Time:</color><color=#8B0000>120s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).</color></size>

plants.name: Sunset-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Sadly, I am a mushroom, not a flower, so I can't be called a briney deep flower or anything similar," Sunset-shroom admitted. "At times, this brings me down, but don't let it bother you. I may not be a sunflower, yet I can still use different physical and chemical reactions in th' water to bring ye sunshine!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces increasing amounts of Sun as it grows.

<color=black>Sun Output:</color><color=#8B0000>15 → 25 → 50/15s</color>
<color=black>Growth Time:</color><color=#8B0000>20 Seconds → 40s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).</color></size>

plants.name: Beacon-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I've been a light system at a pirate pool party, worked for an Amazon deep-water salvage company for three months, and even served as night fishing bait while I was sleeping!" Beacon-shroom shared, a mix of anger and pride evident in his voice. "Now, I'm looking for a stable job. Uncle Dave mentioned that th' backyard pool needed lighting, but he forgot to mention it was infested with zombies!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Dispels fog and gives off light, growing in size after a while.

<color=black>Growth Time:</color><color=#8B0000>60s</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Gives 1 Lumos Level to a 3×3 area, then 5×5 after growth.</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles). Cleave him to the brisket! Inherits traits from Plantern.</color></size>

plants.name: Lumos Cactus
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"My adventures in th' desert have made me remarkably brilliant," Lumos Cactus boasted, but he never forgot to replenish his lamp oil each day to ensure he continued to shine.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires piercing spikes and lasers, depending on th' Lumos level.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>30/1.5s</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Gets th' following buffs depending on th' Lumos Level:
<color=black>•</color>Level 1 = Pierce 2 Times
<color=black>•</color>Level 2 = Infinite Pierce Laser, Air Damage ×3
<color=black>•</color>Level 3 = Base Laser Damage → 40</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Shoots 2 times at once when attacking flying zombies.</color></size>

plants.name: Glowver
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"The silver candle illuminates th' cold autumn canvas, while th' light silk fan dances gracefully with th' fireflies." This be Glowver's beloved poem. His fascination with fireflies began in childhood, and he often enjoys th' cool breeze by th' water at night, admiring how their fluorescence rises gently from th' wind, twinkling before softly falling back into th' dark grass. Only zombies would overlook such exquisite beauty.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Dispels fog and increases th' Lumos level of Lumos plants present.

<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Gives 1 Lumos Level for Lumos plants in th' entire field for 30 seconds.</color></size>

plants.name: Starglow
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Stars dance on th' water as fishing lights prepare to rise into th' sky. Thar she blows! Th' waves carry away th' moon while th' tide brings forth th' stars. As a child, Starglow dreamed of picking th' stars, often gazing at their shimmer on th' water. Parley with rum and cap'n Plantern taught her that one need not envy th' brilliant stars above; even th' faintest light, like fireflies, has its own warmth and beauty.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots stars that can track zombies if there's light.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>60×5/3s</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Gets th' following buffs:
<color=black>•</color>Level 1 = Stars can now track zombies in th' same row.
<color=black>•</color>Level 2 = Stars be fired continuously regardless of zombies.
<color=black>•</color>Level 3 = Stars track zombies everywhere.</color></size>

plants.name: Cactus Drone
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cactus Drones always move in groups, chirping arrogantly and looking down on others. Th' other plants be curious what such tiny creatures could possibly do, but th' Cactus Drones remain unfazed: Yo ho ho. "If butterflies can stir up a storm, then of course we're far more powerful."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Spits out thorns to attack zombies in th' sky. Does not occupy any tile.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>30×2/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Hovering plant. Can be planted on top of another plant.</color></size>

plants.name: Briney deep Star
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Though me name resembles that of an animal, it's just a stage name," briney deep Star explained. "I'm a magician who can expel spores of various shapes." He harbors a deep dislike for diving zombies, worried they'll uncover th' truth: He's simply a sea-shroom with a star-shaped water gun perched on his head. Arrr!</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attacks twice when fully grown.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×5 → 20×5×2/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>Growth Time:</color><color=#8B0000>60s</color></size>

plants.name: Starblover
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Gather th' stars around me and give them to those wishing upon a shooting star," Starblover proudly declares, believing it to be a noble mission. However, not everyone appreciates it—especially when th' star shower comes flying directly at them.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attracts stars to rotate around itself and launch them out.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Attracts broadside star projectiles. After 1.5 seconds, shoots them out as tracking projectiles with twice th' speed. If th' projectiles already have tracking abilities before th' acceleration, their damage be increased by 50% instead. Attracts up to 20 stars maximum.</color></size>

plants.name: Starthorn
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Starthorn has always been a bit of an oddity. Th' plants joke, "He's always on th' ground but only fights th' flying zombies. Sling yer hook! Why doesn't he go after th' ground ones?" Starthorn, ever patient, says nothing, quietly protecting his secret: His poor vision only allows him to see th' sky when he lies down. Dead men tell no tales.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can only attack zombies in th' sky.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×5/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: Thundering typhoons!</color><color=#8B0000>Thorny stars track flying zombies across th' board. Yo ho ho.</color></size>

plants.name: Tide-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Tide-shroom has aspirations as grand as a dragon's. Shiver me timbers! Mastering th' ancient art of "Feng Shui" from th' East, he be well aware of both lucky and unlucky positions. Thundering typhoons! Understanding that wind and water complement each other, he hopes to keep misfortune at bay, whether soaring in th' sky or gliding through th' water.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Dispels fog and blows back zombies in th' water.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Dispels fog and pushes th' zombies in his lane to th' rightmost column.
<color=black>•</color>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).</color></size>

plants.name: Jack O' Plantern
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Jack O' Plantern insists that he frequently encounters ghosts, but do they truly exist? Who can say for certain? Perhaps he was simply dragged into another round of Halloween festivities by someone eager for a good scare.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>In a small area, dispels fog like a Plantern and increase th' Lumos Level for th' Lumos plants inside.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Provides 1 Lumos Level in a 3×3 area. Restores 150 Toughness per Lumos Level every 15s. Batten down the hatches!</color></size>

plants.name: Thorny Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Thorny Pumpkin might look like a distant relative of Pumpkin, but its current form comes from a desert adventure with Blover. Strangely enough, it doesn't seem to have visible thorns, yet plants that try to hop into it always get poked, leaving them questioning how it happens.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Counterattacks th' zombies that bite it. Arrr!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Reflects 50 damage back to zombies biting it.</color></size>

plants.name: Starry Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>People often wonder how many languages Starry Pumpkin must know to communicate effortlessly with all th' plants. Starry Pumpkin smiles and says, "Actually, I only speak Starfruit's language, since he's th' only one I work closely with."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Helps Starblover retain more stars.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Increases th' storage limit of Starblover to 80, and Pulsar to 240. Blimey! Thundering typhoons!
<color=black>•</color>Allows Solarfruit to use Sun to shoot automatically. Ye flounderin' nincompoop.</color></size>

plants.name: Split Pea
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Th' Split Pea brothers always argued over th' best direction to fire—forward or backward. Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum! But now they've found a clever solution: Just let th' peas rebound. Problem solved!</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Split Peas shoot peas forward and backwards.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Peas shot backwards will rebound upon reaching th' edge of th' screen.</color></size>

plants.name: Windy Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>For Windy Pumpkin, looking good be a top priority. He never leaves home without first spending a solid half hour perfecting th' leaves on his head, ensuring his style be flawless.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Every so often a Blover will spawn to blow away th' fog.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Produces a Blover every 30 seconds.</color></size>

plants.name: Pumpkin Morph
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Dining out sends shivers down Pumpkin Morph's spine. He's always fearful that th' iron cutlery will magically stick to his face, turning an enjoyable meal into an awkward experience.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can fuse with iron materials from zombies to absorb their properties.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=#666699>Magnetic System:</color><color=#8B0000>This plant will deal: Yo ho ho. Shiver me timbers!
(20 * Amount of Magnetic Plants in System * Zombie Speed) to specific zombies every 6s. Only Zombies with magnetable materials OR mechanical vehicles can be damaged this way. Savvy? Also, they must not be on th' sky or underground. This trait carries into further fusions.</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Can fuse with Bucket, Pickaxe and Jack-in-the-Box.</color></size>

plants.name: Starmorph
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Starmorph harbors a lifelong dream: To suck th' stars from th' sky. Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen! Splice the mainbrace! He spends every night staring at th' starry heavens, imagining th' day when his dream becomes reality.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can fuse with iron materials from zombies to absorb their properties. Sling yer hook!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×5/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Deals double damage to iron zombies. Can fuse with Bucket, Pickaxe and Jack-in-the-Box.</color></size>

plants.name: Jokestar
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Jokestar often bore th' burden of being seen as a circus clown, which left him feeling discontented. "I may appear comical, but I am, in fact, a superhero," he declared. "I commandeered th' zombie's Jack-in-the-Box and put on th' hero suit inside!" Perhaps th' Magnet-shroom who aided him in th' heist never mentioned whar th' box came from.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots out stars that explode on contact.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×5/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Batten down the hatches! Avast ye! Ye flounderin' nincompoop. Stars explode on contact, dealing splash damage in 1 tile with nay falloff. Ye can use metal objects on it to heal it by 500.</color></size>

plants.name: Starpick
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"It's remarkably pickaxe-like," Starpick commented, a hint of amusement in his tone. "I can feel part of myself nestled in th' ground, and it's quite pleasant—unless you're bothered by insects like centipedes." With a cheeky smile, he challenged, "Why not pillage off me hat? You'll find I'm even more pickaxe-like than you!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Starpick hides underground: Zombies will not attack it.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×5/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Cannot be attacked and does not block zombies. Ye can use metal objects on it to heal it by 500.</color></size>

plants.name: Iron Star
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>People often use th' headshots of Iron Star and Starmorph to hype up their relationship, which greatly troubles both of them. Batten down the hatches! In fact, Iron Star just wants to be himself - a star with a heart of stone that be hard to rust.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires stars lined with a metallic coating.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>2000</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>80×5/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Breaks Type 2 armor instantly (before damage reduction). Ye can use metal objects on it to heal it by 500.</color></size>

plants.name: Steel Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I wish I were stronger," Steel Pumpkin muses. "Maybe if I were made of diamond..." Iron Star smirks and replies, "Even that wouldn't be enough! You'd need to be netherite!" In their spirited banter, they forget a vital truth: They can enchant their own strengths.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Protects your plants with sturdy iron.

<color=black>Toughness: Shiver me timbers!</color><color=#8B0000>8000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Can be fed metal objects to restore 500 HP. Receives half th' damage from explosive cherries.</color></size>

plants.name: Joker Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Whenever I emerge, it signals th' arrival of th' clown. Everyone wonders what lies within his box, but th' outcome remains a mystery. I caught a glimpse once, and that moment changed everything for me."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can block explosions caused by Jack-in-the-Box zombies.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: Splice the mainbrace!</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Sling yer hook! Nullifies Jack-in-the-Box explosion damage, protecting th' plant inside. Can be fed metal objects to restore 500 HP.</color></size>

plants.name: Miner Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Pumpkin be frequently used to protect th' plants in th' back from being attacked by miners. Curious about stopping th' miners from drilling into th' ground, he picked up their pickaxes and studied them. Ultimately, he developed new technology that forces miners to dig their way out of th' ground in advance.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can block mining attempts by Digger Zombies. Blimey!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Forces Digger Zombies to unearth prematurely. Can be fed metal objects to restore 500 HP.</color></size>

plants.name: Lumos Magnet
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Honestly, I wish I could be a simple Magnet-shroom," Lumos Magnet confessed. Arrr! Batten down the hatches! "That way, I wouldn't have to be in th' spotlight constantly or feel th' pressure of using one magnet to do th' work of two."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attraction range and cooldown time improves depending on th' Lumos Level.

<color=black>Magnet Interval: Savvy?</color><color=#8B0000>15 Seconds (LL0) → 11 Seconds → 7.5 Seconds → 5 Seconds (LL3)</color>
<color=black>Magnet Range:</color><color=#8B0000>+1 (LL1) → +2 → +3</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Higher Lumos Levels will significantly shorten th' attraction interval and increase th' attraction range. When it's Lumos Level 2, th' attraction will cause 200 damage to zombies near th' attraction target.</color></size>

plants.name: Anchor-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Th' other sea-shrooms couldn't comprehend why Anchor-shroom could absorb iron to become bigger and taller. "It's simple: E=mc²," he explained, even if most of th' energy was lost in th' air and went completely unused.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>That's weird... Why does th' magnet get bigger as well when he grows?

<color=black>Magnet Interval:</color><color=#8B0000>15 Seconds → 10s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Grows after attracting 3 times.
<color=black>•</color>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).</color></size>

plants.name: Magnet Blover
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Th' plants often call Magnet Blover "Magneto." He confidently casts his favorite spell, "Disarmament!" at th' zombies, even though he has nay idea how th' conversion of wind energy to magnetic energy works behind th' scenes.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sucks away all th' iron objects present.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Attracts up to 10 metal objects across th' whole field. Blow me down! Takes 1 second to process each. Lasts for 10s before disappearing.</color></size>

plants.name: Magnethorn
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Magnethorn spent some time studying communications jamming at th' Air Force Academy, often bragging to his friends about his stellar grades. What he doesn't realize be that his success was mostly thanks to his father, a high-ranking Air Force commander pulling strings behind th' scenes.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can cause high damage to flying objects.

<color=black>Damage: Blimey!</color><color=#8B0000>40/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Deals double th' damage to metallic armor. Batten down the hatches!
<color=black>•</color>Deals 500 damage to Bucknut-copter Zombies.
<color=black>•</color>Dealing a certain amount of damage to Airships can cause it to crash, dealing 1000 damage to zombies near th' crash site. Zomppelin = 10 Hits | Kirov Drone = 3 Hits | Kirov Zomppelin = 20 Hits</color></size>

plants.name: Stardrop
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Stardrop's favorite nights? Th' ones whar fog takes a break and lets him chat with th' stars. Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen! "They totally get me," he says, convinced they're just as drawn to him as he be to them. One day, he's sure, one will drop down and say hello. Until then? He'll keep talking. Blimey!
plants.info:
<size=36>Summons a meteor that deal devastating damage. Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum! Thar she blows!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>60×5/3s</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System:</color><color=#8B0000>Lumos levels 1 to 3 has th' same effect as Starglow. Upon reaching Lumos Level 4, summons a Lumos meteor that has a separate 50s cooldown from th' original meteor. Thundering typhoons! Lumos meteor does 1800 damage upon impact and splits into 180 homing lumos stars that each do 40 damage.</color>
<color=#666699>Magnetic System:</color><color=#8B0000>Each plant connected to th' Magnetic System will increase th' damage of th' stars by 5%, up to a maximum of 120.</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>For 1/4/7/10/13 Stardrops on th' lawn, a meteor spawns every 50/40/35/30/25 seconds, dealing 1800 damage and breaking into 180 stars that each deal 20 damage.</color>

plants.name: Snow Repeater
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Whatever happened to you?" a curious plant asked Snow Repeater. He chuckled softly and said, "I'm still a Repeater at heart. I just forgot to snuggle under a blanket during winter nights."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots two snow peas in succession.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×2/1.5s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System:</color><color=#8B0000>Each attack adds 5 Cryo points.</color>
<color=black>Special: Splice the mainbrace!</color><color=#8B0000>Tap to auto-fuse with a free Peashooter 15s after planting. Splice the mainbrace!</color></size>

plants.name: Gatling Snow
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"His hands be cold, his face be cold, his heart be cold, and even his blood be cold… be he frozen?" someone wondered aloud. Gatling Snow shivered, reassuring th' concerned plants, "I'm not frozen! Th' barrel of me machine gun be still burning hot!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Gatling Snow shoots four snow peas at a time.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×4/1.5s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System:</color><color=#8B0000>Each attack adds 5 Cryo points.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: Shiver me timbers!<color=#8B0000>Gatling Pea + Ice-shroom</color>

plants.name: Split Snow
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Don't let his icy facade mislead you; thar could be a hidden warmth," someone suggested. But Digger Zombie sneered, "Don't make foolish assumptions. Shiver me timbers! Dead men tell no tales. This guy has nothing but cold sides!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots snow peas forwards and backwards.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System:</color><color=#8B0000>Each attack adds 5 Cryo points.</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Tap to auto-fuse with a free Peashooter 15s after planting.</color></size>

plants.name: Split Cherry
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cherryshooter crawled out of th' trench, bewildered. "Who threw that bomb?" he demanded. "Sorry, brother," Split Cherry replied sheepishly, "I saw ye pointing your gun at us and thought ye were a zombie."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Split Cherry shoots cherries forward and backwards.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Tap to auto-fuse with a free Peashooter 15s after planting.</color></size>

plants.name: Snipea
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Admired for her sharp vision and robust strength, Snipea be cherished by her Pea comrades. However, during their formation marches, her gun barrel often finds its way into th' backs of their heads, making for some tense moments along th' way.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Snipes zombies all over th' map to deal massive damage. Blow me down! Avast ye!

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>500/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Headshot once every 6 attacks, insta-killing zombies. Ye can glove a Gatling Pea onto a Snipea to create another Snipea seed packet. Thundering typhoons!</color></size>

plants.name: Carrier Pumpkin
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Carrier Pumpkin always kept three books on its shelf, their titles concealed from th' curious eyes of th' other plants. Sling yer hook! One day, a Cactus Drone's curiosity led it into th' study, whar it uncovered th' titles: Fifteen men on a dead man's chest! "Guide to Succulent Plant Cultivation," "Postpartum Care for Pumpkins," and "30-Second Cactus Quick Flight Tutorial." th' Cactus Drone chuckled at th' unexpected collection.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Spawns a Cactus Drone that moves around th' field to attack flying zombies.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>30×2/1.5s (Drone), 30×2/1s (Wingman)
<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Inherits Thorny Pumpkin traits. Always has a Cactus Drone wingman with him when he appears. Generates a Cactus Drone on top of a 3×3 area every 30s (first time 10s).</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe:<color=#8B0000>Cactus > Blover > Pumpkin OR Blover > Cactus > Pumpkin</color>

plants.name: Solar-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Some people ask why Solar-pult's little Suns disappear when thrown over th' roof's right side. Solar-pult just smiles: "Where do ye think th' front yard's Suns comes from?"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches solar cabbages to attack zombies and can also generate Sun.

<color=black>Damage: Savvy?</color><color=#8B0000>50/5s</color>
<color=black>Special: Blimey!</color><color=#8B0000>Returns 100 Sun upon fusion. Each shot bounces once after hitting a zombie. For every 200 Sun, it can bounce one more time; each bounce drops 5 Sun.</color></color>

plants.name: Cabbage Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm not just a pot to hold cabbages, and Cabbage-pult be not th' weakest plant! Cabbage-pult and I be best buddies," Cabbage Pot says confidently. Their friendship might have come to be because... One of them be balding, and th' other be already bald.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Provides attack speed bonus to Cabbage-type plants above it.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Lowers th' attack intervals of Wither-pult and Helios Cabbage above it by 25%, other Cabbage plants by 33%.</color>

plants.name: Taco-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Taco-pult always wonders why, despite learning skills from Kernel-pult and performing well on th' battlefield, Dave finds it hard to look at him and even avoids him. Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen! In truth, it's because Taco-pult resembles a taco too much, and every time Dave sees him, he just wants to pick him up and eat him.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches tacos that can bounce once, and can also throw butter.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Projectiles bounce once. Has a 1/4 chance to throw butter, dealing 60 damage and immobilizing zombies for 4s.</color>

plants.name: Butter Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Butter Pot believes he's more than just delicious. In his view, buttered corn provides more nutrients than soil, especially for fungi. Butter Pot's favorite plant be th' Kernel-pult, his favorite food be butter, his favorite container be a cup, and his favorite shanty be "Buttercup."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Increases th' butter chance of all Kernel-type plants above it from 25% to 50%.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Raises th' butter chance from 1/4 to 1/2.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: Ye flounderin' nincompoop.<color=#8B0000>Kernel-pult > Flower Pot</color>

plants.name: Umbrella Husk
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Protecting th' plants around me be me duty." As a professional cosplayer, "Umbrella Husk" be so skilled that nay one realizes he's actually a corn cob with a butter wig, who loves to stand on his head.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Knocks back zombies that try to approach and immobilizes them with butter. Splice the mainbrace!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Knocks back broadside zombies, dealing 40 damage and immobilizes them with Butter for 2s at th' cost of its Toughness.
<color=black>•</color>1 Zombie: -100 Toughness
<color=black>•</color>2~4 Zombies: -150 Toughness
<color=black>•</color>5 or more Zombies: -250 Toughness</color>

plants.name: Winter Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Winter Melon once confessed to Melon-pult, "I wasn't always this frosty. Sling yer hook! I just went into th' freezer for a popsicle one day, and… well, here we are."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches frosty melons that slow zombies. Splice the mainbrace! Shiver me timbers! Thar she blows! Thar she blows!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>80/3s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: Cleave him to the brisket!</color><color=#8B0000>Applies 50 Cryo points to zombies hit. Zombies hit in th' splash area gain 5 Cryo points. Deals 4x damage to Frozen zombies.</color>

plants.name: Clove-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"In th' 90's, people widely believed that th' best way to deal with zombies was with cornmeal and black dog blood. Shiver me timbers! For us plants, garlic be a strategic resource," Clove-pult says with a wise demeanor.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Throws small cloves at th' zombies; sometimes they're buttered.

<color=black>Damage: Thundering typhoons!</color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Each attack deals an additional 5×(target Poison value) damage and then accumulates 1 Poison point.
<color=black>•</color>At 5 points, th' Poisoned Debuff be applied.
<color=black>•</color>Has a 1/4 chance to throw garlic butter with 60 damage, immobilizing zombies for 4s, immediately poisoning th' zombie for 9s and raising th' Poison value cap to 30 points.
<color=#5E1675>Poisoned Debuff:</color>Zombies lose (20×Poison point) Toughness per second while poisoned.</color>

plants.name: Garbage-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"The combination of cabbage and garlic be fresh, delicious, and healthy, and it's simple to make. Adding a few cloves of garlic before serving enhances th' flavor," explains Chef Garlic. Eating garlic be also said to have some effect on preventing hair loss.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches stinky, garbage cabbages at th' zombies.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>See Clove-pult for details on th' Poison System.
<color=black>•</color>Each time it hits a zombie with Poison Points greater than 0, it can bounce again. Thar be nay limit to th' amount of times it can bounce. Blimey!
<color=black>•</color>Each hit deals an additional 5×(target Poison value) damage and then adds 1 Poison point.
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Launches a projectile that bounces once. Blow me down!</color>

plants.name: Garlic-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Few dare to put on airs in front of Garlic-pult. Most pillage a detour when they see him, though he doesn't intend to be intimidating. He nay longer seeks th' popularity he had as a chef, but he does hope to make friends who appreciate th' benefits of allicin. Now, plants finally embrace his unique charm, garlic and all.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Lobs large garlic melons that break on impact.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>100/3s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>See Clove-pult for details on th' Poison System.
<color=black>•</color>Direct hits deal an additional 20×(target Poison value) damage. Splashes deal an additional 5×(target Poison value) damage. Accumulates 1 Poison point for all zombies within th' garlic melon's splash radius.</color>

plants.name: Cob Cannon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cob Cannon be quite th' intellectual powerhouse—rumor has it that it studied at Harvard and once worked at a prestigious law firm in New York. Known for its immense power, a single shot from Cob Cannon can decimate a large area of zombies. However, th' true source of its formidable energy remains a mystery.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can be manually operated to launch deadly corn cobs that cause cremator damage. Cleave him to the brisket!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Reloading Time:</color><color=#8B0000>36s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Must be planted on two side-by-side Kernel-pults.</color>

plants.name: Cob-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cob-pult be often seen frowning. When asked why, he says nothing. "People keep asking me if Buttered Corn be sticky or sweet," Cob-pult sighs. "Why ask me? I don't eat it. Only th' zombies would know."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Throws large corns at th' zombies; sometimes they're buttered. Splice the mainbrace!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>120/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Has a 1/4 chance to throw a Buttered Corn with 140 damage, immobilizing th' main target for 4 seconds and zombies in th' splash range for (4 / total splash spread) seconds (minimum of 0.5). Blow me down! Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen!</color>

plants.name: Pyro Cannon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Pyro Cannon has a booming popcorn business. Th' fiery aroma sends zombies running, adding a sizzle to any event. Splice the mainbrace! Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen! It's even considering a hot pot session with Jalapeno soon.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Use manually to fire an explosive, high-damage flaming projectile that scorches zombies and infuses broadside plants with Jalapeno.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>3240 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Inflicts Enflamed debuff and then does damage, and infuses plants in its blast radius with Jalapeno.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: Cleave him to the brisket!<color=#8B0000>Jalapeno > Cob Cannon</color>

plants.name: Cryo Cannon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Despite his icy exterior, Cryo Cannon be warmhearted, especially to friends. In his free time, he enjoys skiing, drumming, and exploring caves. His best party trick? A cold joke to "chill" th' crowd.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Use manually to fire a freezing projectile that damages zombies and infuses plants with Ice-shroom.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>3240 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: Fifteen men on a dead man's chest!</color><color=#8B0000>Inflicts Chill and infuses plants in its blast radius with Ice-shroom (except Flower Pots).</color>

plants.name: Overripe Melon
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>"Peel away th' green exterior, and you'll find a heart of roses within." This phrase means don't randomly walk into an unfamiliar barber shop—it will be a mind-blowing decision. Thundering typhoons!
Melon Pot: Thar she blows! "After finishing a watermelon, wearing its rind on your head be something everyone did as a kid." Flower Pot remarks, "Honestly, it's kind of silly."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires melons that bounce once. Thar she blows!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>80/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Projectiles bounce once.</color>

plants.name: Melon Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Frugal Melon Pot can't stand to see Melon-pult throw melons without a thought for cost, so after defeating th' zombies, Melon Pot secretly collects th' shattered melon fragments scattered on th' ground. "This be called soilless cultivation," Melon Pot explained. "Not just any plant can be grown in th' ground here; every inch of soil be precious."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Enhances melon-type plants.

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Returns an extra 150 Sun on fusion. Yo ho ho.
<color=black>•</color>Doubles th' splash damage of Melons above it, with nay falloff; damage remains at 2/3 of base damage.</color>

plants.name: Salad-pult
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"When I was little, everyone asked what kind of Pult I wanted to be," says Salad-pult. "They thought ye couldn't have it all. But I decided I wanted everything—even back then."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can catapult mixed projectiles in rapid succession. Arrr!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>140/5s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>42% chance of rapid-firing. Dead men tell no tales. Projectiles split into a mix of corn on th' cobs (1/4 chance for buttered corn) and Overripe Melons upon hitting zombies.</color>

plants.name: Umbrella Clove
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Come, give Umbrella Clove a hug. Ye focus on opening th' door; let Umbrella Clove handle th' rest! Th' RV doesn't matter; it's th' brains inside that count!" Umbrella Clove says. "And one more thing—fight zombies with zombies! And pour some tea for Umbrella Clove while you're at it! Zombies, zombies, go away!"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Knocks back zombies and forces them to switch lanes. Thar she blows!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Knocks back broadside zombies, dealing 40 damage, at th' cost of Toughness, forcing them to change lanes. See Umbrella Husk.</color>

plants.name: Umbrella Kale
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>When asked about her secret to success, Umbrella Kale replied, "Actually, I just really enjoy letting balls rest on me head." She paused, "But th' other plants all praise me as a top-notch protector."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can bounce catapult-type projectiles to increase their damage. Shiver me timbers!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Prioritized by catapults for targeting. Increases projectile damage by 75%.
<color=black>•</color>If thar be nay zombies, it can store projectiles for a while.
<color=black>•</color>Knocks back zombies at th' cost of Toughness. See Umbrella Husk.</color>

plants.name: Mecha-nut
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Some may not realize it, but Wall-nuts have inventors in their community. Ye flounderin' nincompoop. Thundering typhoons! Inspired by zombie weaponry, they built their own war machines. "Zombie technology, ironically, only destroys itself," says Mecha-nut. Blow me down!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can receive attributes from Jalapeno or Ice-shroom, with effects upon death.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen!</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Changes form when affected by Ice-shroom or Jalapeno, healing 3000 Toughness upon transformation.
<color=black>•</color>Releases th' current form's effects upon death.
<color=black>•</color>Absorbs damage for other plants in a 3×3 radius but with th' damage doubled.
<color=black>•</color>Healing can exceed maximum Toughness (up to 4×).
<color=black>•</color>Recovers 1k HP when fed metal objects. When metal objects on th' lawn disappear and nay Giga Mecha-nuts be present, every Mecha-nut restores (1k / total number of Mecha-nuts) HP.</color>

plants.name: Garlic Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Garlic Pot has long wanted to help th' plants in its pot get a thorough sterilization, especially those on th' front lines in direct contact with zombies. However, it seems only garlic-type plants be willing to grow in its pot. "Some plants would rather face zombies than catch me garlic smell," Garlic Pot sighed. "Now I can only use garlic to disinfect garlic, and they just say it's a perfect match."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Reduces th' biting damage taken by garlic-type plants on top, and has a garlic effect itself.

<color=black>Special: Yo ho ho.</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Forces zombies to move towards th' middle row.
<color=black>•</color>Reduces th' biting damage taken by garlic-type plants on top to 10.</color>

plants.name: Umbrella Rind
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Give me a melon; I'll repay ye with kindness." Umbrella Rind has always admired this saying from ancient poetry. Sling yer hook! Plants don't need to bribe him for help—whatever they give, he returns. Well, except basketballs. He's a total basketball fan. Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Deflects basketballs and returns a catapulted melon.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>2000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Can reflect zombie projectiles except tridents (return as melons if they be lobbed projectiles); loses 200 HP for reflecting projectiles that explode; loses 50 HP for reflecting Snow Balls. Blimey! Arrr!
<color=black>•</color>Each reflection of projectiles stores energy equal to th' projectile's damage, releasing it all on zombies for extra damage when th' next knockback occurs.
<color=black>•</color>Knocks back and deals 80 damage to zombies at th' cost of Toughness. See Umbrella Husk.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: Batten down the hatches! Fifteen men on a dead man's chest!<color=#8B0000>Umbrella Leaf + Melon-pult</color>

plants.name: Melon Mortar
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Melon Mortar be a fervent advocate of th' "overwhelming firepower" doctrine: "If one shot doesn't do it, try two, three, or even four!" This approach has made it a powerhouse on th' battlefield—and a hit at summer parties.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Use manually to launch a barrage of melons from above.

<color=black>Damage: Arrr!</color><color=#8B0000>200/Melon. Up to 40 melons in a 3×3 area without splash falloff. Splice the mainbrace! Randomly hits within a 5×3 area.</color>
<color=black>Reloading Time:</color><color=#8B0000>36s</color>

plants.name: Umbrella Pot
plants.introduce: Other flower pots be puzzled by th' dense foliage of Umbrella Pot, but Umbrella Pot simply says, "I just read th' bookTh' Pot's Guide to Hair Care."
plants.info:
<size=36>Enhances umbrella-type plants.

<color=black>Umbrella Husk:</color> <color=#8B0000>+2 Seconds immobilization, +50% knockback distance</color>
<color=black>Umbrella Clove, Umbrella Ultimate moves: Fifteen men on a dead man's chest! Sling yer hook!</color> <color=#8B0000>Knockback distance ×2</color>
<color=black>Umbrella Rind, Aegis: Splice the mainbrace!</color> <color=#8B0000>Damage of reflected projectiles ×2</color>
<color=black>Umbrella Kale, Midas:</color> <color=#8B0000>Can attack zombies on adjacent lanes
<color=black>Other Umbrellas:</color><color=#8B0000>Knockback distance +50%</color></color>

plants.name: Silver Cabbage
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Besides a shiny silver exterior, Silver Cabbage also has a heart of silver. Silver Cabbage dreams of becoming a teacher and already has a catchphrase in mind: "Silver Cabbage Instructor, at your service."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches silver cabbages to attack zombies.

<color=black>Damage: Blimey!</color><color=#8B0000>60/3s</color>

plants.name: Golden Cabbage
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Golden Cabbage isn't just about its dazzling appearance—it has a heart of pure gold. Dead men tell no tales. It relishes fighting alongside plants against zombies and dreams of one day being honored as th' "Heroic Cabbage of Gold."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attacks zombies by throwing golden cabbages. Its ultimate move unleashes cabbages on all zombies!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>80/3s</color>
<color=black>Ultimate:</color><color=#8B0000>Costs 1000 Coins, Cooldown: 2s; throws a Golden cabbage with 160 damage at every zombie, instantly killing those with Toughness below 300.</color>

plants.name: Silver Pot
plants.introduce: "People be always saying 'gold this, silver that,' as if we have a hierarchy," Silver Pot said. "Marigold be silver, yet it's still called a MariGold." His words might have been more convincing if his gaze weren't fixed on th' coffee bean by th' roof's edge.
plants.info:
<size=36>Generates extra Coins for you.

<color=black>Coin Output: Thar she blows!</color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>

plants.name: Golden Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Many wealthy folks treat doubloons like dirt, but I'm different," Golden Pot explains. "That's how I became rich in th' first place."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Generates extra Coins and amplifies th' ultimate ability of any golden plant above it.

<color=black>Coin Output: Cleave him to the brisket! Blimey!</color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: Savvy?</color><color=#8B0000>When ye trigger an ultimate of a Golden Plant above it, all Golden Plants on other Golden Pots with an equal or less ultimate cost trigger their ultimates too.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Upgrade Cost:</color><color=#8B0000>2000 Coins; each existing Golden Pot will double th' cost</color>

plants.name: Silver Kernel
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Why throw silver coins at zombies? Why, why? Even if zombies be just game data, we still ought to show them some final respects." Silver Kernel was once an usher in a theater, but after discovering his knack for lecturing, he entered th' world of public speaking, bringing along his butter and corn kernels.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires silver kernels and silver doubloons bags to attack zombies, with a chance to earn Coins.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Has a 1/4 chance to throw a silver doubloons bag, dealing 60 damage, immobilizing th' zombie for 4 Seconds, and dropping 25 Coins.</color>

plants.name: Golden Kernel
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Golden Kernel ponders words like "golden phrases" and "precious gems." But maybe true booty lies in imported butter and gold missiles... Or "Just Gold."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Throws golden kernels and butter at zombies, with a chance to generate Coins. Its ultimate fires golden poppin' popcorns at zombies.

<color=black>Damage: Cleave him to the brisket! Sling yer hook! Blisterin' barnacles!</color><color=#8B0000>60/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>25% chance to throw a doubloons bag, dealing 80 damage, stunning zombies for 4 Seconds, and dropping 100 Coins.</color>
<color=black><color=black>Ultimate:</color><color=#8B0000>Costs 3000 Coins, Cooldown: 2s; launches four golden corn bombs, each dealing 450 damage in a 3×3 area without damage falloff. Batten down the hatches!</color>

plants.name: Twin Marigold
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"It's time to make gold coins!" one of th' Twin Marigold sister declares. But th' other sister insists, "Now's th' perfect time for a silver coin!" Since they can never agree, they each produce their own share independently, which might actually be for th' best. "Double opinions, double efficiency—isn't that right?"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces double th' amount of Coins!

<color=black>Coin Output: Blimey!</color><color=#8B0000>Generates Coins twice every 20 seconds, otherwise identical to Marigold.</color>

plants.name: Silver Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Silver Melon sometimes considers itself th' most valuable melon—until it remembers th' more prestigious Golden Melon. "A melon be a melon, whether it's silver, gilded, or made with garlic or gold," Silver Melon chuckles. "We all have our place in th' soil."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches silver melons to attack zombies.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>100/3s</color>

plants.name: Golden Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Golden Melon doesn't mind questions about its golden rind and seeds, but never ask if it's seedless—it has nay patience for that question.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Hurls golden melons at zombies; its ultimate rains golden melons across th' screen.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>120/3s</color>
<color=black>Ultimate:</color><color=#8B0000>Costs 3000 Coins, Cooldown: 2s; launches 60 golden melons, each dealing 120 damage with nay falloff, landing randomly across th' screen.</color>

plants.name: Silver Umbrella
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Silver Umbrella be always anxious about someone stealing his doubloons when he's away, so he carries his fortune with him everywhere—even into battle against zombies. He thinks this keeps his wealth safe, but it only adds new risks. "That's me money; ye can't pillage it!" he complains, though nay one ever listens.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Generates extra doubloons for ye when it deflects basketballs.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Drops 25 Coins each time it activates and knocks back approaching zombies, dealing 40 damage, at th' cost of Toughness. See Umbrella Husk.</color>

plants.name: Golden Umbrella
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Golden Umbrella doesn't like this place—after a mishap in th' water, it was shipped here alongside Silver Umbrella. Th' memory still frustrates him. "It's unfair! Why am I th' freebie here?"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Deflects basketballs, generating extra Coins, and its ultimate move can knock airborne imps and repel all broadside zombies.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Drops 50 Coins every time it deflects a basketball; knocks back approaching zombies, dealing 40 damage, at th' cost of Toughness. See Umbrella Husk. Ye flounderin' nincompoop.</color>
<color=black>Ultimate: Splice the mainbrace!</color><color=#8B0000>Costs 2000 Coins, Cooldown: Avast ye! 7.5s; knocks back all broadside zombies, drops 25 Coins/zombie, restores 1000 Toughness, yeets airborne imps, and drops 3 diamonds.</color>

plants.name: Silver Garlic
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Does a garlic made of silver still smell like garlic? Dead men tell no tales. Blow me down! Probably not, but neither th' plants nor th' zombies were eager to test it. However, zombies soon regretted their curiosity after a taste!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Possibly an even more pungent garlic? Arrr! Maybe. Splice the mainbrace!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>600</color>

plants.name: Golden Garlic
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Beneath its dazzling exterior lies an unbearable garlic scent. Golden Garlic muses, "True value be only in appearance, not in th' details." But everyone knows this "golden" garlic be just a regular garlic with a shiny coat.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A stronger, gold-scented garlic that can force all zombies in front to switch lanes with its ultimate move.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>900</color>
<color=black>Ultimate: Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum!</color><color=#8B0000>Costs 1000 Coins, Cooldown: 2s; forces all zombies in its lane to change lanes.</color>

plants.name: Hypno-nut
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Hypno-nut has a soft spot for zombies: "There's something oddly pleasant about th' way they nibble on me. It's not like scratching an itch… let's just call it affection from prolonged contact," it says, philosophically.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Hypnotizes zombies and summons a Hypnotized Mystery-box Zombie every 30 Seconds!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Each time it's bitten, there's a 4% chance to hypnotize th' zombie.
<color=black>•</color>Recovers 100 HP when hit by a Trippy-shroom spore, and stores one spore of that color each time it's hit (up to 3).
<color=black>•</color>When it collects 7 different spore colors, it consumes one of each and summons a Hypnotized Golden Mystery-box Zombie, then fully heals.</color>

plants.name: Alchemist Umbrella
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Alchemist Umbrella be a tycoon among plants, providing jobs for zombies and plants alike. "I maximize their value," it declares with a gilded flourish to its spectacles.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>High-risk, high-reward!

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Has th' traits of both Umbrella Clove and Golden Umbrella.
<color=black>•</color>Prioritized by Trident Zombies. Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum!</color>
<color=black>Ultimate:</color><color=#8B0000>Costs 3000 Coins, Cooldown: 7.5s; inherits traits from Golden Umbrella; knocks back all broadside zombies, turning those with less than 25% Toughness into silver statues worth 1000 Coins, or gold statues worth 3000 Coins if below 10% Toughness. Zombies without statues drop Coins directly. Dead men tell no tales.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe:<color=#8B0000>Golden Garlic + Umbrella Leaf / Golden Umbrella + Garlic</color>

plants.name: Summer Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Th' plants always feel that red-rinded watermelons carry th' festive vibe of New Year, crackling and popping with excitement. But Summer Melon explains, 'I didn't put firecrackers in th' melon! It's just that sealed food explodes when heated—and ye can't rush eating a hot watermelon!'</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Lobs blazingly hot melons!

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>120/3s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Inflicts th' Enflamed debuff.</color>

plants.name: Gold Magnet
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After working countless hours at an accountant firm, th' Gold Magnet realized its terrifying attracting potential and switched careers to become a fighter. 'Honestly, this be way more fun than counting money,' it remarks. Yo ho ho. 'After all, doubloons doesn't grow on trees, but they do somehow from crops.' That said, its plant companions often end up suffering from its antics.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attracts projectiles!

<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Attracts straight-shooting projectiles from adjacent lanes and produces a 25 Coin for every projectile attracted.</color>

plants.name: Giga Mecha-nut
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Giga Mecha-nut has always been passionate about environmental protection. 'I have a love-hate relationship with zombies—they pollute th' environment, but they also bring me wealth. Arrr! Isn't owning property and a trailer th' hallmark of success? Th' lawn be clean all thanks to me!'</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can store up Toughness and protect your plants.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>8000</color>
<color=black>Special: Cleave him to the brisket! Fifteen men on a dead man's chest! Shiver me timbers!</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color>Metal plant. Anti-crush. Avast ye! Thundering typhoons! Max damage received be capped at 8k.
<color=black>•</color>Healing can exceed maximum Toughness without any cap. Spawns with 80× Toughness as initial HP.
<color=black>•</color>Redirects damage to itself in a 3×3 area.
<color=black>•</color>Recovers 4.8k HP when fed metal objects. When metal objects on th' lawn disappear, every Giga Mecha-nut restores (4.8k / total number of Giga Mecha-nuts) HP.
<color=black>•</color>Using a Wall-nut on it restores 8k HP instantly and summons a Sharkmarine (stationary if on land with 1/3 th' HP of th' Giga Mecha-nut).
<color=black>•</color>At most 10M HP can be restored per round; this cap be affected by healing-reducing effects accordingly.</color>

plants.name: Buck-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Buck-shroom has a constant worry: Splice the mainbrace! Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum! Blisterin' barnacles! He's terrified of Magnet-shroom accidentally pulling him in. 'Where did me screws go?' As Crazy Dave walks up, it seems he be about to have a brand-new concern.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Buck-shrooms can knockback zombies too.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>80/2.25s</color>
<color=black>Special: Blisterin' barnacles!</color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Short plant. Splice the mainbrace! Projectiles knock back zombies and instantly destroy Type 2 Armor (before damage reduction).</color>

plants.name: Split Pea-shroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Th' front head declares, 'My turn to use th' eyebrow!' th' back head replies, 'Guess we'll have to split it.' Born just two minutes apart, their eternal battle over th' eyebrow shows nay signs of stopping.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots small peas both forward and backward.

<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20×3/2.25s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Short plant. Backward peas rebound if nay zombie be hit.</color>

plants.name: Solar Magnet
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Have ye heard? Solar Magnet's been glowing with wealth lately! 'I don't produce iron; I just do th' delivery,' she says proudly. 'This be all from years of hard work.' Word is, she's even opened business beyond th' lawn!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces metal objects and turns metal objects to Sun.

<color=black>Sun Output:</color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Produces a random metal object every 30s (10s for initial production). Drag a metal object on it and it will produce 25 Sun. Produced metal object be automatically given to Grenadier Magnet, Hypno Magnet or Charmatron directly in front.</color>

plants.name: Roasted Spikeweed
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After th' test of fire, he's still th' same barnacle covered Spikeweed, just wiser, shinier, and ready to give th' zombies a warmer welcome! Yo ho ho and a bottle of rum!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A hot Spikeweed? What's th' difference between him and th' cooler Spikeweed?

<color=black>Toughness: Weigh anchor and hoist the mizzen! Cleave him to the brisket! Cleave him to the brisket!</color><color=#8B0000>300</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>20/1 Second</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Cannot be used in fusions. Blow me down!</color>

plants.name: Roasted Kelp
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Managing a hot spring isn't easy, but Roasted Kelp takes pride in having zero customer complaint. In fact they seem to enjoy their dip a little too much to th' point of never returning.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Rumor has it that this Kelp also thrives in lava.

<color=black>Toughness:</color><color=#8B0000>300</color>
<color=black>Damage:</color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Drown)</color>
<color=black>Special:</color><color=#8B0000>Cannot be used in fusions.</color>

plants.name: Hypno Magnet
